ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for 'grain bowl with egg and toppings, no sauce'. Mapped to closest grain bowl food product concept. Exact multi-component customisable bowl not represented in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKM (Mixed dishes based on cereals) is the closest defensible anchor. No specific code exists for customisable grain bowl with egg and four vegetable/protein toppings without sauce.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept for this customisable grain bowl preparation.

main_ingredients: cooked grains (brown rice, quinoa, or mixed grains)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 g, representing a typical grain bowl with egg and four toppings as served at Singaporean health-food stalls and cafes.

nutrient_source.title: Grain bowl with egg (4 toppings), no sauce — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Grain bowl with egg (4 toppings), no sauce' (cr_id: F08010035). All per-100g values preserved as provided. Saturated fat estimated at ~0.8 g/100g based on HPB egg and grain composition data for this dish variant; monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans fat, potassium, cholesterol, and micronutrient fields not individually reported in HPB source and set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed grain and legume dishes, analogous estimate for grain bowl with egg
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific customisable grain bowl. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on mixed whole-grain dishes (brown rice GI ~50, quinoa GI ~53, mixed grain bowl with vegetables and protein GI ~40–50) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021. The no-sauce variant reduces added sugar contribution, supporting a lower GI estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 10.89 × 3.5) / 100 = 45 × 38.1 / 100 ≈ 17.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 145 mg/100g — well below the 400 mg/100g high-sodium threshold
hypertension.guidance: This grain bowl with egg and four toppings, served without sauce, provides only 145 mg sodium per 100g (approximately 508 mg per 350g serving), making it a suitable low-sodium meal option for hypertensive individuals; patients should ensure protein toppings are not pre-marinated in high-sodium sauces to maintain this benefit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycaemic index (~45), moderate carbohydrate 10.89 g/100g, protein 4.46 g/100g, dietary fibre 1.82 g/100g
type2_diabetes.guidance: The combination of a low estimated GI (~45), moderate carbohydrate content, 4.46 g protein, and 1.82 g dietary fibre per 100g produces a low glycaemic load (~17 per 350g serving), making this bowl a clinically appropriate meal for type 2 diabetes management; selecting a whole-grain base and adding legume toppings will further attenuate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.8 g/100g), low sodium (145 mg/100g), high dietary fibre relative to energy density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: With approximately 0.8 g saturated fat and 145 mg sodium per 100g, and a caloric density of only 91 kcal/100g, this grain bowl supports a heart-healthy dietary pattern; the egg contributes dietary cholesterol but the overall saturated fat and sodium profile remains favourable for cardiovascular disease risk management when consumed as part of a balanced diet.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium 145 mg/100g well below 400 mg RA threshold; egg provides vitamin D; vegetable toppings contribute antioxidants
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The low sodium content, egg-derived vitamin D, and antioxidant-rich vegetable toppings make this bowl suitable for rheumatoid arthritis patients; toppings should favour cruciferous vegetables and anti-inflammatory spices, and nightshade vegetables should be avoided if RA symptoms are active.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grain Bowl with Egg (4 Toppings), No Sauce is a customisable, health-oriented mixed dish popular in Singapore's contemporary food scene, consisting of a cooked grain base (typically brown rice, quinoa, or a mixed-grain blend), one egg (commonly boiled, poached, or soft-cooked), and four user-selected toppings such as leafy greens, roasted vegetables, edamame, tofu, or lean protein. The absence of sauce distinguishes this variant from sauced versions, eliminating added sugar and sodium from dressings. Per 100g, the dish provides 91 kcal, 4.46g protein, 3.34g fat, 10.89g carbohydrate, 2.69g sugar, 1.82g dietary fibre, and 145mg sodium, representing the highest protein content among the grain bowl variants in the HPB Singapore database. The low glycaemic index (~45) and moderate carbohydrate content produce a low glycaemic load per serving, making this bowl clinically appropriate for type 2 diabetes management, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease risk reduction. The egg contributes vitamin D and high-quality complete protein, while the four vegetable toppings add micronutrients, antioxidants, and dietary fibre. This dish is suitable for vegetarians (when egg is included), halal diners, and those following gluten-free or dairy-free diets. It is commonly consumed as a lunch or dinner meal in Singaporean health-food cafes, workplace canteens, and hawker-adjacent stalls offering customisable bowls.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F08010035), which is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and consistent. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature, clearly documented. Confidence is high for nutrient data; GI is an approved analogous estimate.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available in HPB source): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. These detailed fatty acid, micronutrient, and moisture data are not individually reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this customisable grain bowl variant as of 2026. Saturated_fat_g estimated at 0.8 g/100g based on HPB compositional data for egg and grain components in this dish variant; this is an approximation and should be confirmed if precise saturated fat reporting is required. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 350g serving = (45 × 38.1) / 100 ≈ 17.1. All core macros (protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) are non-zero and sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification required. Dietary tags include low_calorie as per input record.
